summary: Verified Vercel build safety-net configuration for date-fns pin. All four config files present and correct. Both npm --dry-run and yarn install completed with exit code 0 in isolated temp copies (no ERESOLVE, no date-fns@4 anywhere). Runtime regression on preview URL clean: home-page renders, rate ticker + contact form + collections + showroom galleries all present, 0 console errors.

verification_details.yarn_lock: Only date-fns@3.6.0 entry at line 4757. Zero references to date-fns@4.x (grep count = 0).
verification_details.npm_install_dry_run: Exit 0, 1495 packages, adds date-fns 3.6.0. Zero ERESOLVE errors.
verification_details.yarn_install_fresh: Exit 0 in 103.61s. node_modules/date-fns/package.json version = 3.6.0. Only one date-fns directory installed (no duplicates). Warnings shown are pre-existing resolution mismatches and peer dep warnings, not fatal.

action_items: User must push /app/frontend/package.json, /app/frontend/vercel.json, /app/frontend/.npmrc, /app/frontend/yarn.lock to GitHub via Emergent's 'Save to GitHub' UI so Vercel picks up the new configuration. Once pushed, Vercel will use yarn (per vercel.json) which honors yarn.lock + resolutions and installs date-fns@3.6.0 deterministically.
critical_code_review_comments: Safety-net configuration is triple-redundant and correct: yarn side pinned via resolutions + yarn.lock, npm side pinned via overrides + .npmrc legacy-peer-deps, Vercel forced to yarn via vercel.json. Under any package manager Vercel picks, date-fns@3.6.0 is enforced.
Recommend keeping packageManager field yarn@1.22.22 to further hint Vercel/corepack.
No code issues discovered during regression.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 confirmed the date-fns pin holds under both npm and yarn install simulations run in /tmp temp copies. The frontend preview at https://patna-jewels-1.preview.emergentagent.com is running cleanly. Next agent: no action needed unless a new Vercel build failure is reported after user pushes changes to GitHub.
